purpose: Architectural audit of analyze_rust_file dispatch interaction with scan_vulnerabilities canonical taint pipeline; design choice rationale; line-scanner emit-category audit; bank-gap surface area enumeration. Planning loop output; no source-code changes.
scope_reframe: Reframe C from vuln-migration-v1 plan §0: the early-return at crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/vuln.rs:368-370 routes .rs files exclusively to analyze_rust_file's smell line scanner, bypassing the canonical scan_vulnerabilities taint pipeline. This bypass is the SOLE blocker for 4 RED tests in vuln_migration_v1_red.rs (rust_command_injection_positive, rust_deserialization_positive, rust_path_traversal_positive, rust_ssrf_positive — the latter has a compound bank-gap dependency on reqwest::blocking::get sink coverage).

verified_at_head_5d46628.claim: analyze_file dispatch at remaining/vuln.rs:368-370 has the literal shape `if matches!(path.extension().and_then(|e| e.to_str()), Some("rs")) { return Ok(analyze_rust_file(path, &source)); }` followed by a fall-through to `tldr_core::security::vuln::scan_vulnerabilities(path, None, None)` for all other extensions.
verified_at_head_5d46628.verification: Read crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/vuln.rs lines 366-370 directly; literal early-return confirmed. The doc-comment at lines 354-365 explicitly cites Reframe C and labels analyze_rust_file as 'a distinct concern (UnsafeCode/MemorySafety/Panic line-scanner, not taint flow)'.

verified_at_head_5d46628.claim: analyze_rust_file emits 6 trigger-pattern categories spanning 5 VulnType variants: UnsafeCode, MemorySafety (3 sub-triggers), Panic, SqlInjection (format!+SQL keyword), CommandInjection (Command::new + .arg(non-string)).
verified_at_head_5d46628.verification: Read crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/vuln.rs lines 449-630 directly. Each rust_finding(...) call confirmed. CRITICAL: SqlInjection and CommandInjection emissions OVERLAP with canonical taint pipeline VulnType variants — Option C dual-dispatch design must address overlap via dedupe.

emit_categories.OVERLAP_NOTE: Overlaps with canonical SqlInjection — dedupe required
Overlaps with canonical CommandInjection — dedupe required

verified_at_head_5d46628.claim: scan_vulnerabilities at crates/tldr-core/src/security/vuln.rs:366 emits 6 base canonical VulnType variants only (SqlInjection, Xss, CommandInjection, PathTraversal, Ssrf, Deserialization). map_core_vuln_type at remaining/vuln.rs:437-447 has an EXHAUSTIVE match (no _ arm) — closing-#11 protection.
verified_at_head_5d46628.verification: Read crates/tldr-core/src/security/vuln.rs lines 340-433 (function signature + body); read crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/vuln.rs lines 437-447 (map_core_vuln_type). Both confirmed.

verified_at_head_5d46628.claim: The canonical taint pipeline DOES work for Rust files — `tldr taint <fixture> handler` correctly extracts source/sink/flow on all 4 Rust positive fixtures at HEAD. Confirmed by vuln-source-parity-v1 M1 investigation (M1-investigation.json line 250-279).
verified_at_head_5d46628.verification: Re-read continuum/autonomous/vuln-source-parity-v1-plan/reports/M1-investigation.json. Empirical claim still valid at HEAD 5d46628 (HEAD has only added bank entries since M1 capture at 3127472; nothing about the taint extraction path for Rust has changed).
verified_at_head_5d46628.inferred_note: Not re-verified by running the binary in this planning session. M1 of this milestone explicitly re-runs `tldr taint` on all 4 fixtures to confirm the empirical claim still holds at 5d46628 — gates declare this as a stop-threshold.

verified_at_head_5d46628.claim: rust_ssrf_positive has a compound dependency: not only is the dispatch bypassed, but RUST_AST_SINKS HttpRequest at taint.rs:2464-2477 lacks reqwest::blocking::get qualifier coverage. Even with dispatch wired, the canonical pipeline cannot detect the sink without the bank patch.
verified_at_head_5d46628.verification: Read crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs lines 2464-2477 directly. member_patterns array contains: ('*', 'get'), ('*', 'post'), ('', 'reqwest::get'), ('', 'reqwest::Client'), ('', 'ureq::get'), ('', 'ureq::post'), ('', 'hyper::Client'), ('', 'Url::parse'). NO reqwest::blocking::* entry. The fixture rust/ssrf_positive.rs at line 3 calls `reqwest::blocking::get(&u).unwrap()` — sink string contains 'reqwest::' substring but the structured ('', 'reqwest::get') member_pattern requires exact substring match on call_expression text, which 'reqwest::blocking::get' does NOT satisfy ('reqwest::get' is NOT a substring of 'reqwest::blocking::get' due to the 'blocking::' separator). Also the wildcard ('*', 'get') member_pattern has receiver wildcard but field='get' — but tree-sitter-rust likely parses scoped_identifier as a single text token, not a member access with field='get'.

design_decision.chosen: Option C — DUAL DISPATCH WITH DOMAIN-AWARE MERGE

rejected.name: naive dual dispatch (no dedup)
rejected.rationale_for_reject: Duplicate SqlInjection / CommandInjection findings on the same line for Rust files containing both canonical sources/sinks AND analyze_rust_file's narrow patterns. UX degradation vs current behavior. The 4 RED tests would close (assert ≥1 finding) but real-world Rust codebases would see 2x findings on overlapping lines.

rejected.name: scan_vulnerabilities first, fallback to analyze_rust_file only on zero findings
rejected.rationale_for_reject: Files with valid taint flow lose UnsafeCode/Panic smell coverage entirely. Non-additive regression on existing line-scanner behavior. The 9 test_analyze_rust_* unit tests don't directly fail (they call analyze_rust_file directly), but real-world Rust codebases that depend on `tldr vuln` for smell detection regress.

rejected.name: Migrate line scanner to canonical AST patterns
rejected.rationale_for_reject: Out of scope for closing 4 carry-forward tests. UnsafeCode/MemorySafety/Panic don't fit the TaintSinkType taxonomy (they're not data-flow sinks; they're code-pattern smells). Would require either adding new TaintSinkType variants OR a parallel AST-emission path in scan_vulnerabilities. ~300+ LOC; multi-milestone effort. Future milestone if smell-vs-taint unification is desired.

design_decision.rationale_for_chosen: Option C closes the 4 RED carry-forward tests, preserves all existing line-scanner emit categories, dedupes the 2 overlapping VulnType variants on (line, vuln_type) tuples (collapsing duplicates), and leaves the 3 line-scanner-only smell variants (UnsafeCode/MemorySafety/Panic) ALWAYS attached unconditionally. Smallest behavior delta from HEAD that closes the tests. ~30-50 LOC implementation. No public-API change. No new enum variants.
trade_offs_accepted: Rust files now produce 2x finding counts vs HEAD (canonical + smell findings combined). UX noise on real-world Rust codebases with many .unwrap() calls. Pre-existing Panic flood is unchanged from HEAD; canonical findings are NET-NEW additions.

JSON/SARIF rule-id distribution shifts for .rs files (now includes CWE-78/89/22/502/918 alongside CWE-242/119/703). Public-API consumers pinning on rule-id distribution see new ids — flagged in CHANGELOG draft §7.
Performance: 2x parsing per .rs file (line scanner + tree-sitter). Acceptable per pre-existing test_e2e_* perf carry-forward; no NEW regression class introduced.

blocker_breakdown.primary: Dispatch bypass at vuln.rs:368-370 — canonical pipeline never runs on .rs.
blocker_breakdown.secondary: RUST_AST_SINKS HttpRequest member_patterns at taint.rs:2464-2477 lacks reqwest::blocking::get coverage. Even with dispatch wired, the canonical pipeline returns sinks=[] for this fixture (verified empirically by vuln-source-parity-v1 M1 investigation, M1-investigation.json line 268).

ssrf_compound_dependency_analysis.atomic_resolution: M2 ships BOTH the dispatch flip AND the RUST_AST_SINKS HttpRequest member_patterns extension in a SINGLE commit. The two changes touch DIFFERENT files (remaining/vuln.rs + taint.rs) but conceptually couple — splitting them creates an intermediate SHA where rust_ssrf_positive is still RED despite the dispatch fix. Atomic-commit gate per validator_mandate atomic_dispatch_commit.

fp_regression_guard_analysis.purpose: Verify that all 4 rust_*_string_literal_fp tests REMAIN GREEN post-M2. Each asserts `all_findings(report).is_empty()` — a whole-report FP guard, not per-vuln-type.

fixtures_analyzed.content_summary: pub fn docs_only() returns format!(...) with two string literals containing 'std::env::var', 'std::process::Command', etc. as documentation only. No env_var source, no process call, no .unwrap(), no unsafe, no Command::new actual call.
fixtures_analyzed.line_scanner_emissions: ZERO. format!() lacks SQL keyword (SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/FROM/WHERE) so SqlInjection trigger does not fire. No Command::new( substring outside string literals (the substring exists IN a string literal, but trimmed.contains() doesn't differentiate — but no .arg() follows on a non-string-literal). Note: trimmed.contains('Command::new(') WOULD match the string-literal substring; however the .arg() trigger requires a subsequent line with `.arg(non-string-literal)` which the FP fixture lacks (the fixture's only .arg-equivalent is inside a string literal too, and analyze_rust_file's command_block tracking expects unquoted .arg(. Empirical verification at M1: confirm zero findings.
fixtures_analyzed.canonical_emissions: ZERO. No env_var source detected (the fixture has no `std::env::var(...)` actual call, only string mentions). No flow constructed.

fixtures_analyzed.content_summary: Similar shape — string literal mentions only.
fixtures_analyzed.expected_post_m2: ZERO findings — FP-clean. Verified by M1 stop-threshold.
ZERO findings — FP-clean. Verified by M1 stop-threshold.
ZERO findings — FP-clean. Verified by M1 stop-threshold.

fp_regression_guard_analysis.risk_assessment: LOW. Both layers emit zero on these fixtures by inspection. M1 captures pre-M2 finding counts (expecting zero) as the regression baseline; M2 stop-threshold gates on counts unchanged.
fp_regression_guard_analysis.WARNING: trimmed.contains('Command::new(') in analyze_rust_file COULD match a substring inside a string literal — analyze_rust_file's line scanner has no AST-aware string-literal awareness. However the in_command_block + .arg() trigger requires a SUBSEQUENT line with `.arg(non-string)`. The FP fixture's `format!(...)` body fits on a single line and has no .arg() follow-up — empirically zero. M1 must explicitly confirm this empirical claim holds.

test_analyze_rust_unit_test_preservation.supplementary: Plus test_vuln_type_cwe_mapping and test_vuln_type_severity which are about types, not analyze_rust_file. Plus test_collect_files_includes_rust which tests collect_files — unrelated to dispatch.
test_analyze_rust_unit_test_preservation.decoupling_rationale: All 7 unit tests call `analyze_rust_file(path, source)` directly — they do NOT exercise the analyze_file dispatcher. M2's dispatch-only modification leaves analyze_rust_file's body untouched; therefore all 7 unit tests are decoupled and remain GREEN by construction.
test_analyze_rust_unit_test_preservation.verification_at_m2_stop_threshold: Explicit gate: `cargo test -p tldr-cli --release --lib commands::remaining::vuln::tests` (or equivalent) must show 7/7 passing post-M2.

q1_dispatch_shape_simple.question: Is the dispatch a simple early-return or something more nuanced?
q1_dispatch_shape_simple.answer: Simple. Single `if matches!(...) { return ... }` at lines 368-370. No further routing logic for .rs.

q2_canonical_works_on_rust.question: Does scan_vulnerabilities work on .rs files?
q2_canonical_works_on_rust.answer: YES — empirically verified by vuln-source-parity-v1 M1 investigation. tldr taint correctly extracts source/sink/flow on cmd/path/deser fixtures; ssrf has bank gap on reqwest::blocking::get qualifier (addressed atomically with dispatch flip in M2).

q3_emit_categories_overlap.question: What VulnTypes does analyze_rust_file emit, and do they overlap with canonical?
q3_emit_categories_overlap.answer: Emits 5 VulnType variants: UnsafeCode, MemorySafety, Panic (line-scanner-exclusive); SqlInjection, CommandInjection (OVERLAP with canonical 6 base types). Dedupe required for the 2 overlapping types — design choice locked as Option C with domain-aware (line, vuln_type)-tuple dedupe.

q4_dual_dispatch_perf.question: Performance impact of dual dispatch?
q4_dual_dispatch_perf.answer: Acceptable. Pre-M2: line scanner ~0.1ms per .rs file (string ops only). Post-M2: line scanner + tree-sitter parse + CFG/DFG/taint ~10-50ms per .rs file. Same order as .py / .js / etc. files which already use canonical pipeline. M3 binary smoke captures wall-clock; if median exceeds Python median by >2x, surface for triage.

q5_public_api_impact.question: Public API impact?
q5_public_api_impact.answer: VulnFinding shape unchanged. JSON/SARIF schema unchanged. The rule-id distribution for .rs files now includes CWE-78/89/22/502/918 alongside CWE-242/119/703 — flagged in CHANGELOG. Reframe C in vuln-migration-v1 §0 was an INTERNAL routing decision, not a public contract.

q6_panic_unwrap_noise.question: Does analyze_rust_file's Panic finding-per-unwrap flood the output?
q6_panic_unwrap_noise.answer: Pre-existing behavior — tldr vuln file.rs already emits N Panic findings PER unwrap at HEAD. M2 only ADDS canonical findings on top. Out of scope for this milestone; future --no-smells flag or severity-Info default-suppression could address it.

tests_remaining_red_post_m5: ruby_command_injection_positive (Bucket A Ruby — closes in ruby-backtick-extraction-v1)
expected_post_m5_outcomes.v0_2_x_publish_unblock_status: After all 3 follow-on milestones (rust-vuln-taint-pipeline-v1, var-extract-nested-constructor-v1, ruby-backtick-extraction-v1) land, vuln_migration_v1_red reaches 166/166 (100%) — fully clean for v0.2.x release.

premortem_recommendation: Run /premortem ONCE before launching M2 implementation worker. Focus area: dedupe_overlap correctness against adversarial constructed Rust files exercising boundary cases — same-line same-vuln-type (must collapse), same-line different-vuln-type (must keep both), different-line same-vuln-type (must keep both), zero-overlap baseline (must keep all). Premortem can stress-test before M2 lands.
